$comment: OpenTurbine hardware-in-the-loop bench rig — single source of truth for the wiring, shared by the OTBench tester firmware and the PC harness. DUT = ESP32-S3 running OpenTurbine. Tester = ESP32 classic running OTBench firmware. Edit pins here to match your physical harness; the firmware table (bench/firmware/src/main.cpp) MUST be kept in step with the tester_gpio/tester_kind values below.

wiring_rules: Common ground between the two boards is mandatory.
Put a 470-1000 ohm resistor in series with EVERY cross-link wire — cheap insurance against push-pull contention if a pin direction is ever misconfigured.

No smoothing capacitors available, so the only clean analog channels are the classic ESP32's two true DACs (GPIO25/26): THROTTLE_IN and OILP. FLAME (a threshold sensor) and IDLE_IN are driven as plain digital HIGH/LOW instead of a swept voltage.
Tester board constraints honoured: GPIO 36/39 unavailable; input-only pins 34-39 not used; GPIO 16/17 avoided (PSRAM on WROVER modules); strapping pins 0/2/5/12/15 avoided.
If IDLE_IN is left unwired, tie the S3 idle-input pin to GND so its ADC does not float.

signals.name: START
signals.notes: value 1 = pressed (tester drives LOW); 0 = released (tester Hi-Z, DUT pull-up)

signals.name: N1
signals.notes: tester generates a square wave; harness converts rpm to Hz using ppr

signals.name: OILP
signals.notes: true DAC, 0-3.3 V; assert on oil_raw counts (raw = volts/3.3*4095)

signals.name: FLAME
signals.notes: threshold sensor: HIGH (3.3 V) = above threshold, LOW = below

signals.name: IDLE_IN
signals.notes: SERVO_OUT: SET IDLE_IN <microseconds> emits a 50 Hz RC pulse for RC-PWM input testing (set DUT idle_input rc_pwm=true on GPIO5); tie GPIO5 to GND if not wired

signals.name: N2
signals.notes: governor testing; DUT GPIO8 (27 is internal flash on S3); tester GPIO18 freed when THROTTLE_OUT moved to 17

signals.name: THROTTLE_OUT
signals.notes: 1000-2000 us at 50 Hz; DUT moved 16->40 (this S3's GPIO16/17 do not drive), tester moved 18->17

signals.name: STARTER_OUT
signals.notes: only meaningful if OT_HAS_STARTER enabled on the DUT

signals.name: OILPUMP_OUT
signals.notes: ~10 kHz; harness compares measured duty to oil_pct/100

signals.name: IGNITER
signals.notes: if the DUT igniter is set to PWM dwell mode, change tester_kind to pwm_in_ledc

dut_profile.$comment: Set these on the OpenTurbine Hardware web page (or flash a matching hardware_profile.h) so the S3 pins line up with dut_gpio above. verify-wiring compares GET /api/hardware against this.

idle_input.note: driven digital or tied to GND
